node_proposals.title: Example Forecast Lab
node_proposals.summary: 11-service AI platform for synthetic forecasting, built from zero as design engineer and sole technical founder (2025–present). Web: Next.js 15, React 19, Apollo GraphQL, Tailwind, Clerk auth. Native mobile: SwiftUI (iOS), Kotlin/Jetpack Compose (Android), RevenueCat payments. Conversational AI via LangGraph with multi-model routing (OpenAI, Anthropic, Groq) and SSE streaming tool calls for live synthetic scenarios. Proprietary factor-discovery prediction engine (XGBoost, LightGBM) with three-window temporal validation and market-aware evaluation after the ~4.5% vig. Decision automation on NautilusTrader + Kalshi with Redis-backed control state, circuit breakers, daily P&L monitoring. Real-time data pipeline from ESPN, SportsGameOdds, BallDontLie; dbt analytics with Dagster orchestration. Research tool 'Example Research Shell' (terminal agent on Bun with WhatsApp gateway). Results: positive ROI across every synthetic forecast type (improved calibration across all synthetic forecast categories), validated on 1,000+ synthetic evaluations. 50 pilot users, weekly active cohort, no paid acquisition.

node_proposals.title: Example Studio
node_proposals.summary: Creative technology studio — Principal + Founder since 2021. One senior builder, augmented by AI, embedded with client teams. many products shipped, many launch impressions. Engagements span AI transformation, frontend architecture, design systems, brand identity, and zero-to-one product. Philosophy: software as an architectural problem; brand, architecture, and interface as one discipline.

attributes.operating_model: One senior builder + AI, embedded with client team

node_proposals.title: Example Data Studio (2025)
node_proposals.summary: AI Transformation & Frontend Architecture engagement under Example Studio. Embedded with 11-person engineering team. Designed complete UI/UX for a browser-based SQL IDE. Built 66-component design system on Radix/shadcn/ui with Tailwind CSS 4. Monaco Editor integration with multi-dialect SQL tokenizers. Coached the team from 50 commits/week to 300+ sustained (high velocity increase).

node_proposals.title: Example Care Journal (2025)
node_proposals.summary: Conversational Health AI, sole designer-engineer. Designed a 7-phase clinical conversation arc (Mirror → System Explanation → Reframe → Keystone Action → What NOT to Do → Tracking Prompt → Anchoring Truth). Built a 5-category persistent memory system and multi-store RAG across four vector stores. Three persona modes: Guide, Deep Dive, Practitioner. early waitlist. Stack: Next.js 15, React 19, Python/FastAPI, OpenAI Agent Kit, Mem0, Redis, PostgreSQL.

node_proposals.title: Example Market (2024)
node_proposals.summary: Co-founder. HR Consultant Marketplace — CRM + marketplace for independent HR consultants. Led product strategy from 80 user interviews through launch. Designed client workspaces, cross-client dashboard, marketplace, invoicing. Built brand identity and investor materials. Raised small pre-seed round.

node_proposals.title: Example Wellness (2025)
node_proposals.summary: Luxury wellness brand engagement. Brand identity, web experience (Framer), physical space integration. Dark-and-gold palette, infinity moonmark logomark. Took the brand from zero patients to six weeks fully booked.

attributes.outcome: Zero patients to six weeks fully booked

node_proposals.title: Example Logistics (2024)
node_proposals.summary: Logistics Compliance SaaS. Rebuilt the design system with engineering-grade structure — semantic tokens, 1:1 Figma-to-code mapping. Co-designed rules-engine taxonomy for ML-powered invoice auditing. Redesigned onboarding from a 2-month human process into a 2-week software pipeline. Clients include True Classic, Magic Spoon, Seed Health, Nood.

node_proposals.title: Example Golf (2024)
node_proposals.summary: Sports & commerce platform across three surfaces: consumer mobile (GPS, shot tracking, 8+ game modes), on-course ordering with cart GPS delivery, and a web admin with POS integration. Designed for direct sunlight readability and one-handed use. Recognized at SXSW; Alberta Innovates grant recipient.

node_proposals.title: Example HR (2017–2024)
node_proposals.summary: Director, Product Design. Joined as employee #2 at 5 people with seed funding. Grew over 6 years to Director of Product Design reporting to CEO. Built entire customer-facing product from scratch (onboarding, compliance management, employee records, HR policy library, communication hub, termination compliance workflow) and internal HR manager platform for 200+ HR managers. Designed conversion funnel, adaptive onboarding, 5-tier pricing system. Built 'Example Design System' design system. Produced investor materials supporting staged capital raised across seed–Series C. Managed 3 designers. Company: $0 → growth-stage revenue, growth-stage valuation, 5 → 200 employees, 0 → 10,000 businesses.

node_proposals.title: Example Care (2014–2016)
node_proposals.summary: Co-Founder / Designer + Engineer. Designed and engineered the first veterinary telehealth platform — category creation before the category existed. Native iOS (Objective-C), native Android (Java), provider web portal (Rails), real-time video on TokBox/OpenTok with reconnection handling for 3G/4G. 20,000+ users, press in Forbes and Yahoo Finance. Mapped the VCPR regulatory boundary that shaped every pet telehealth platform that followed.

node_proposals.title: Example Arts Festival (2012–2013)
node_proposals.summary: Head of Design. Won a competitive brand pitch against 5 agencies at age 24. Created the iconic paint-splattered heart logo. Designed 10,000+ pieces of environmental signage across 18 city blocks of downtown Las Vegas. Built custom CMS + API layer syncing website, mobile app, and artist management. Designed stage graphics, merchandise, maps, wristbands. large attendance on opening day; the festival still runs a decade later.

node_proposals.title: Example Audio
node_proposals.summary: Creator discovery platform (co-founder). Responsibilities: strategy, branding, UI, UX, code.

node_proposals.title: Example Labs
node_proposals.summary: Environmental testing dashboards. Complex data visualization under IE9 constraints.

node_proposals.title: Example Events (2013)
node_proposals.summary: Platform for the electronic music community to promote and discover events and festivals.

node_proposals.title: Example Fitness
node_proposals.summary: Home fitness / AI platform. Led Data team. Example Data + Braze customer engagement.

node_proposals.title: Design-Engineering Compression
node_proposals.summary: Holding design, engineering, and product in the same head removes an entire class of translation errors — interfaces get better, architecture gets cleaner, and products ship faster. The core intellectual property of the owner's working method and the Example Studio model.

attributes.origin: Evolved through career spanning design and engineering simultaneously

node_proposals.title: Three-Window Temporal Validation
node_proposals.summary: Proprietary prediction methodology used in Example Forecast Lab: frozen temporal boundaries across three evaluation windows with market-aware scoring after the ~4.5% synthetic-forecasting vig. Prevents data leakage and grounds ROI claims in realistic market conditions.

node_proposals.title: AI-Assisted Velocity Coaching
node_proposals.summary: Coaching engineering teams to sustain 5–7x commit velocity increases through AI-assisted development workflows. Demonstrated concretely at Example Data Studio: small-team baseline to sustained high-throughput delivery.

node_proposals.title: Senior-Builder-Plus-AI Studio Model
node_proposals.summary: Example Studio's operating model: one senior builder augmented by AI, embedded with the client team. Only works when that person holds design, engineering, and product together. Small by conviction — the compression is the feature, not a resource constraint.

node_proposals.title: Semantic Design Tokens with 1:1 Figma-to-Code Mapping
node_proposals.summary: Engineering-grade design systems where Figma tokens map directly to code tokens without translation drift. Applied at Example Logistics and Example Data Studio. A concrete expression of the design-engineering compression value.

node_proposals.title: Conversational AI Clinical Arc
node_proposals.summary: 7-phase health conversation design framework developed for Example Care Journal: Mirror → System Explanation → Reframe → Keystone Action → What NOT to Do → Tracking Prompt → Anchoring Truth. Paired with a 5-category persistent memory system and multi-store RAG.

node_proposals.title: Zero-to-One AI Product Build
node_proposals.summary: Own every layer — design, frontend, backend, data pipeline, ML, agent orchestration — as sole technical founder or embedded partner. Demonstrated at Example Forecast Lab (11-service platform, 50 pilot users, no paid acquisition) and Example Care Journal (60,000 waitlist).

node_proposals.title: Embedded Design-Engineering Partnership
node_proposals.summary: Drop into a client team, rebuild the design system with engineering rigor, and coach velocity upward with AI-assisted workflows. Demonstrated at Example Data Studio (11-person team, high velocity) and Example Logistics (onboarding from 2 months to 2 weeks).

node_proposals.title: Software as an Architectural Problem
node_proposals.summary: We think about software the way architects think about light — where it enters, how it moves, what it reveals. The interface is a consequence of that thinking, not the starting point. The foundational Example Studio stance.

node_proposals.title: Brand, Architecture, and Interface Are One Discipline
node_proposals.summary: Most teams fracture brand, architecture, and interface across departments. Example Studio holds them as one. This unification is the source of compression advantages.

node_proposals.title: Small Studio by Conviction
node_proposals.summary: Not a resource constraint — a product decision. Compression is the feature. The single senior builder + AI model produces better outcomes than larger teams with translation layers.

node_proposals.title: Prediction-Engine IP Path
node_proposals.summary: What is the right long-term vehicle (product, license, platform) for the factor-discovery + temporal-validation methodology proven on NBA props? The IP could stay inside Example Forecast Lab, be licensed, or become a standalone platform.

attributes.question: What is the right long-term vehicle (product, license, platform) for the factor-discovery + temporal-validation methodology proven on NBA props?

node_proposals.title: Studio Scale Ceiling
node_proposals.summary: How far can the senior-builder-plus-AI model scale before craft degrades or another model is needed? Open question about the ceiling of compression and what lies beyond it.

attributes.question: How far can the senior-builder-plus-AI model scale before craft degrades or another model is needed?

node_proposals.title: Agentic Architecture Maturity
node_proposals.summary: Which agentic patterns (LangGraph vs. OpenAI Agent Kit vs. Anthropic Agent SDK) are durable vs. transient as the tooling shifts? An active technical inquiry across all AI product work.

attributes.question: Which agentic patterns (LangGraph vs. OpenAI Agent Kit vs. Anthropic Agent SDK) are durable vs. transient as the tooling shifts?

edge_proposals.rationale: the owner is the sole technical founder and design engineer of Example Forecast Lab, having built every layer from zero.
the owner is the Principal and Founder of Example Studio since 2021.
the owner was Director of Product Design at Example HR from 2017–2024, joining as employee #2.
the owner co-founded Example Care and served as designer and engineer from 2014–2016.
the owner was Head of Design at Example Arts Festival in 2012–2013.
the owner co-founded Example Audio and led strategy, branding, UI/UX, and code.
the owner built environmental testing dashboards for Example Labs.
the owner worked on Example Events, a music event discovery platform, in 2013.
the owner led the Data team at Example Fitness.
Example Data Studio was a Example Studio client engagement.
Example Care Journal was built by the owner as sole designer-engineer under Example Studio.
Example Market was a co-founder / Example Studio engagement.
Example Wellness was a Example Studio engagement.
Example Logistics was a Example Studio engagement.
Example Golf was a Example Studio engagement.
The senior-builder-plus-AI model is Example Studio's explicit operating model.
Design-engineering compression is the foundational capability that makes the senior-builder-plus-AI studio model viable.
Example Forecast Lab's prediction engine implements the three-window temporal validation methodology.
The Example Data Studio engagement is the primary concrete demonstration of AI-assisted velocity coaching (50→300+ commits/week).
The Example Data Studio engagement produced a 66-component design system using semantic tokens and engineering-grade structure.
Example Logistics engagement rebuilt the design system with semantic tokens and 1:1 Figma-to-code mapping.
Example Care Journal implements the conversational AI clinical arc framework.
Example Forecast Lab is the primary live demonstration of the zero-to-one AI product build playbook.
Example Care Journal is a strong secondary demonstration of the zero-to-one AI product build playbook (sole designer-engineer, 60,000 waitlist).
Example Data Studio is the primary demonstration of the embedded design-engineering partnership playbook.
Example Logistics is a secondary demonstration of the embedded design-engineering partnership playbook (onboarding pipeline compression).
Software as an architectural problem is the foundational philosophical stance of Example Studio.
The stance that brand, architecture, and interface are one discipline directly reinforces the design-engineering compression concept.
The small-studio-by-conviction stance is the philosophical grounding for the senior-builder-plus-AI operating model.
The prediction-engine IP path inquiry is about the long-term future of IP currently housed in Example Forecast Lab.
The inquiry specifically calls out the factor-discovery + temporal-validation methodology as the IP asset in question.
The studio scale ceiling inquiry directly questions the limits of the senior-builder-plus-AI model.
Example Forecast Lab uses LangGraph and multi-model routing, making agentic architecture maturity directly relevant.
Example Care Journal uses OpenAI Agent Kit, making agentic architecture maturity directly relevant.

notes: All 29 entities from the semantic artifact are represented with stable proposal_ids. No new entity IDs were introduced.

page_type assignments: ent-001 through ent-015 → project; ent-016 through ent-021 → concept; ent-022 through ent-023 → playbook; ent-024 through ent-026 → stance; ent-027 through ent-029 → inquiry. No person nodes were created — the synthesis artifact found no named individuals beyond the owner.
The owner node (Example Owner) is not included in node_proposals as it is the existing graph owner identity, not a candidate node. All owner-sourced edges use 'owner' as source_ref per rules.

Two additional worked_on / held_role_at edges from owner to ent-013, ent-014, ent-015 were added beyond the semantic artifact's explicit relationship list, grounded in the upload evidence. These are minor background projects with clear ownership signal.

The open_inquiries slugs (how-should-example-forecast-lab-evolve-beyond-current-subscriber-base-and-trading-stack and where-is-the-next-frontier-for-example-studio-engagements) were intentionally not entityized per the synthesis note: they are less crystallized and the first is captured structurally via ent-027/ent-028. They are preserved only in the owner attributes of the semantic artifact.
The Example Research Shell tool is deliberately kept as an attribute of ent-001 rather than a separate project node, consistent with the synthesis artifact's treatment.

ent-020 (Semantic Design Tokens) relates_to_refs includes both ent-003 and ent-007 as the two primary concrete demonstrations. Example HR/Example Design System is noted in attributes.applied_in but the Example HR engagement predates the explicit concept formalization.
Career break (Oct 2021 – Jul 2022) is preserved in owner attributes within the positioning block of the semantic artifact; it is not a separate node but is cross-referenced in life_priorities.
All evidence_refs on both nodes and edges are passed through exactly as they appear in the semantic artifact. No evidence_refs were synthesized or inferred beyond what the artifact provided.
